description: Manual tuning history log. Append one entry per accepted/rejected micro-batch.

entries.criterionFocus: LR9-2 one-row micro-batch: require sufficient paragraph evidence density before allowing Band-9 lexical error profile outputs

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Stop residual LR=9 overscore on thin-conclusion boundary scripts where LR9-1 already fails but LR9-2 still passes and keeps Band-9 gate alive.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Preserve low-band behavior and keep Band-9 path available when paragraph evidence density is sufficient.

targetSamples.before: overall 8.5 vs examiner 7.5 (+1.0), scores TR=7 CC=8 LR=9 GRA=9 after BATCH-13D

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Step6r + step6q + low-band followup/activation/polarity suites pass after patch.

summaryMetrics.deterministicShift: LR9-2 now returns occasional when paragraphProfile evidence density is thin (minSent <= 1 or insufficient paragraph/body structure).

decision.reason: Patch and guards are green; live rerun confirmation is required.
decision.nextAction: Rerun Cambridge 14/Test 04 with fixed model/provider and confirm LR drops from 9 to 8 and overall returns to ±0.5.

entries.notes: Strict one-row patch only; no scoring formula/aggregation changes.

entries.criterionFocus: LR9-1 and GRA9-1 strict high-band evidence-density guard: avoid Band-9 promotion when paragraph-level evidence is too thin

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Pull overscored 8.5 boundary cases (where LR/GRA both reached 9) back into range by requiring stronger paragraph-level evidence density before awarding Band-9 feature rows.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Preserve low-band behavior and keep high-band pass path when evidence density is sufficient.

targetSamples.before: overall 8.5 vs examiner 7.5 (+1.0), scores TR=8 CC=8 LR=9 GRA=9

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Step6r + step6q + low-band followup/activation/polarity suites pass after patch.

summaryMetrics.deterministicShift: LR9-1/GRA9-1 now return No when paragraphProfile evidence density is thin (minSent <= 1 or insufficient paragraph/body structure)

decision.reason: Patch and guards are green; live rerun confirmation is required.
decision.nextAction: Rerun Cambridge 14/Test 04 with fixed model/provider to confirm LR/GRA drop from 9 to 8 and overall returns to ±0.5.

entries.notes: Strict high-band patch only; no scoring formula/aggregation changes.

entries.criterionFocus: CC9-1 one-row micro-batch: require stronger non-basic connector volume to avoid Band-9 over-credit on boundary scripts

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Prevent CC=9 over-lift when non-basic connector volume is still boundary-level (for example 7), while preserving clear Band-9-safe CC profiles.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Preserve low-band and mid-band stability; keep CC9 pass path for clearly stronger non-basic connector volume.

targetSamples.before: overall 8.5 vs examiner 7.5 (+1.0), with CC=9 and CC9-1=Yes despite CC9-2=No

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Step6r + step6q + low-band followup/activation/polarity suites pass after patch.

summaryMetrics.deterministicShift: CC9-1 now returns No when totalConnectorsExcludingBasic < 8

decision.reason: Patch and guards are green; live rerun confirmation is needed.
decision.nextAction: Rerun Cambridge 20/Test 03 with fixed model/provider to confirm CC drops from 9 to 8 and overall returns to ±0.5.

entries.notes: Strict one-row patch only; no scoring formula/aggregation changes.

entries.criterionFocus: CC3-2 one-row micro-batch: add conservative deterministic coherence/weakness boundary to reduce Step-4 AI dependence

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Reduce row-level variance for CC3-2 on boundary scripts by deterministically resolving clearly coherent and clearly weak cohesion profiles while preserving null for ambiguous cases.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Preserve low-band behavior and fallback capability for unsupported rows.

targetSamples.before: two runs already stable at overall 7.5 vs examiner 7.0 (+0.5)

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Step6r + step6q + low-band followup/activation/polarity suites pass after patch.

summaryMetrics.deterministicShift: CC3-2 now returns No/Yes on clear profiles and null on ambiguous profiles

decision.reason: Patch is guard-green and deterministic coverage improved, but post-patch live rerun is still needed for acceptance.
decision.nextAction: Rerun the same sample with fixed model/provider for at least 3 attempts and confirm reduced CC3-2 AI fallback/variance.

entries.notes: Strict one-row patch only; no scoring formula, aggregation, or Step-3 prompt/schema changes.

entries.criterionFocus: TR9-2 one-row micro-batch: block repetitive single-part two-body over-lift while preserving clean high-breadth path

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Reduce high-band overscore on repetitive single-part scripts where TR9-2 was too permissive despite repetition-heavy discourse.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Preserve previously accepted explanation-led TR9-2 boundary and low-band protections.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Low-band suites remain green after patch.

summaryMetrics.deterministicShift: TR9-2 tightened for repetitive single-part two-body boundary

decision.reason: Target moved into ?0.5 and guard remained stable on fixed rerun.
decision.nextAction: Freeze this micro-batch and continue next unseen checkpoint set.

entries.notes: TR9-2 now blocks Yes for single-part two-body scripts when repetition is elevated (topCount>=8 and ratio>=0.025), preventing boundary over-lift.

entries.criterionFocus: Refactor only: split scoring rules by TR/CC/LR/GRA with shared helper module

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Improve maintainability and safer micro-batching without changing scoring behavior.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Exact behavioral parity with legacy scoring rules.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: No behavior changes intended; all guard suites passed.

decision.reason: Behavior-preserving split verified by key parity and full IELTS guard suite pass.
decision.nextAction: Keep scoringRules.legacy.js temporarily; remove after runtime soak approval.

entries.notes: scoringRules.js is now a thin aggregator wrapper. Legacy monolith retained as scoringRules.legacy.js by request.

entries.criterionFocus: Scoring History bulk category selection consistency

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Prevent partial category assignment caused by page-only selection by expanding category actions to all filtered pages when Select All is active.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No scoring formula or criterion drift.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: UI-only behavior fix; scoring logic unchanged.

decision.reason: Bulk category actions now resolve full filtered selection across pagination and report partial outcomes clearly.
decision.nextAction: Manual UI check with multi-page history set using Select All.

entries.notes: When header Select All is active and total filtered items exceed current page rows, the client now fetches all filtered pages and sends all IDs to category-many endpoint.

entries.criterionFocus: Scoring History bulk clear-category action

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Allow selected scoring-history rows to remove an assigned category in one action for faster recategorization.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No scoring formula or criterion drift.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: UI/data-layer feature only; scoring logic unchanged.

decision.reason: Bulk clear-category flow is wired and validations pass.
decision.nextAction: Manual browser check: select sessions -> Clear Category -> verify row tint/badge removed and Uncategorized filter reflects changes.

entries.notes: Implemented as an extension of existing category-many endpoint via clear=true to keep API surface small.

entries.criterionFocus: Scoring History run categorization UX/data plumbing

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Improve scoring-run triage by allowing bulk category assignment with color and label, plus category filtering.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No scoring formula or criterion drift.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Scoring logic unchanged; UI/data-layer feature only.

decision.reason: Feature delivered with backend + UI wiring and syntax checks passing.
decision.nextAction: Manual browser verification in Scoring History on selected rows.

entries.notes: Category assignment is override-by-design: the newest category replaces previous category on the same sessions.

entries.criterionFocus: CC9-2 one-row follow-up: high-band cohesion ceiling (non-basic connector volume) tightening

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Reduce C20/T03 over-score by blocking CC9 pass when cohesion diversity is strong but non-basic connector volume is still thin (7), capping CC at 8.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No harmful movement on previously accepted mid-band guards and no low-band regression.

decision.nextAction: Rerun target + guards on fixed profile and decide accept/freeze.

entries.notes: CC9-2 now requires at least 8 non-basic connectors to pass Band-9 cohesion ceiling when other Band-9 checks are already met.

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Promote LR6 partial gate for the C20/T01 two-idea single-part boundary profile (adequate/mixed/weak with controlled repetition) by turning LR6-2 from No to Yes under strict guards.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No harmful movement on C20/T04 and C17/T02.

decision.nextAction: Freeze BATCH-12X and proceed to next unseen sample set under the same fixed runtime profile.

entries.notes: Batch frozen after validation export confirmed LR6-2=Yes on target and no harmful guard drift.

entries.notes: BATCH-12W local tests were green but live rerun showed no movement; target remained blocked at LR6 gate.

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Unstick Cambridge 20/Test 01 where extraction shape has 2 ideas and only one strongly-developed body row, while keeping weak/repetition guards strict.

hypothesis.guardExpectation: Keep weak or repetition-heavy profiles blocked through explicit repetition and echo constraints.

decision.nextAction: Rerun target + guards on fixed runtime profile and decide accept/freeze.

entries.notes: Strict one-row patch only. No Step 3/Step 4 mode changes.

entries.criterionFocus: Step 3 consensus extraction shape fix: preserve and vote rich language signals (lexicalControl/grammarControl) to prevent Step 4 fallback inflation

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Prevent large Step 4 AI queue increases after Step 3 consensus by retaining rich language evidence fields required by deterministic row rules.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Single-run Step 3 output remains unchanged; only consensus extraction assembly is corrected.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Not run yet in this pass.

decision.reason: Consensus output now preserves rich language signals; requires fresh sample rerun to verify Step 4 queue size and score consistency against pre-3x behavior.
decision.nextAction: Run one sample in single-run Step 3 and one sample in Step 3 consensus 3x, then compare Step 4 queue counts and criterion deltas.

entries.notes: Added lexicalControl/grammarControl keys to Step 3 signal map and ensured consensus extraction carries these fields forward from voted/base values.

entries.criterionFocus: Step 3 stability consensus runtime optimization: execute 3 extraction runs in backend parallel mode and return run bundle for consensus assembly

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Reduce wall-clock Step 3 consensus latency by running the requested consensus runs concurrently on backend and returning all runs in one response.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Single-run Step 3 path remains unchanged; consensus scoring logic remains unchanged (still based on run bundle).
hypothesis.lowBandExpectation: No low-band scoring-rule regression (runtime orchestration change only).

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Not run yet in this pass.

decision.reason: Backend parallel consensus execution is implemented and locally validated; live runtime comparison on sample runs is required.
decision.nextAction: Run one sample with Step 3 single-run and then Step 3 stability+consensus (3x) to confirm runtime reduction and stable consensus output.

entries.notes: Controller now accepts Step 3 stability mode with runCount and parallel flag, executes run bundle in backend, and returns meta.stabilityRuns for UI consensus assembly.

entries.criterionFocus: v0326 Step 3 dual-mode UX restore: keep Single Run path and keep separate 3x Stability+Consensus path

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Allow operators to choose either normal single-run extraction or explicit 3x consensus extraction without losing existing consensus capability.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No Step 4 scoring-rule or aggregation logic changes; this is Step 3 runtime/UI routing only.

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Not run yet in this pass.

decision.reason: Single-run Step 3 path restored while retaining explicit 3x consensus button and consensus execution path.
decision.nextAction: Run a quick UI smoke on one sample in both modes, then continue full-book runs.

entries.notes: Step 3 consensus button is now visible in v0326 normal mode; main review modal confirms single-run extraction.

entries.criterionFocus: Runtime consistency hardening: force Step 3 execution in v0326 to 3-run stability consensus and use only consensus extraction output

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Reduce run-to-run drift by replacing default single-run Step 3 extraction with enforced 3-run consensus extraction in normal v0326 workflow.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No Step 4 scoring-rule or aggregation logic changes; downstream grading should consume the consensus Step 3 output only.
hypothesis.lowBandExpectation: No low-band rule regression (runtime flow change only).

measuredResult.lowBandSummary: Not run yet in this patch pass.

decision.reason: Step 3 default execution path now delegates to 3-run consensus in v0326; requires fresh sample rerun validation.
decision.nextAction: Run Book 20 target samples with fixed model profile and compare drift against previous single-run behavior.

entries.notes: UI labels now explicitly show 3x consensus. Saved or loaded step3RunCount settings are enforced to 3 during execution and session restore.

entries.criterionFocus: Strict 3-row Book 5 micro-batch on TR8-1, TR8-2, CC7-1 for compact high-control single-part boundary

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Lift Cambridge 05/Test 04 from 8.0 toward 8.5+ by recovering TR8 and CC7 boundary decisions in compact but high-control single-part scripts without broad relaxation.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Keep Cambridge 05/Test 01/02/03 within ±0.5 and preserve existing low-band protections.
hypothesis.lowBandExpectation: No regression in low-band activation/followup/polarity suites.

decision.nextAction: Freeze this phase and move to the next unseen higher-band checkpoint set on fixed profile.

entries.notes: TR8-1/TR8-2 gained compact single-part high-control boundary recovery; CC7-1 gained low-density high-control boundary recovery near densityExBasic 1.5-1.6 with strict support/topic/echo/language guards.

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Saved/exported JSON filenames are easier to identify by including the current sample name while preserving safe filesystem characters.

entries.criterionFocus: TR9-2 one-row deterministic full-development boundary for clear covered/developed responses when support is explanation-led (not strictly example-led)

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Reduce TR9-2 AI fallback volatility and recover Cambridge 06/Test 02 high-band under-score when position is clear, coverage is complete, support is developed, and echo is clean.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: Preserve Cambridge 06/Test 01 exact match and Cambridge 06/Test 03 low-band behavior by keeping strict low-breadth and support-quality guards.

entries.notes: Patch keeps existing compact low-breadth over-lift block intact and only adds a conservative explanation-led full-development deterministic Yes path with strict coverage/support/echo/length constraints.

entries.criterionFocus: CC7-1 one-row high-reference repetition-boundary recovery for compact recoverable high-content profiles

entries.notes: Added high-reference repetition-boundary deterministic pass path and strengthened over-trigger fixture to use non-stopword repetition so the guard remains strict.

entries.criterionFocus: Step-4 runtime consistency: preserve complete Step-2 taskEcho payload instead of recomputing and drifting lexical boundary decisions

hypothesis.expectedEffect: Prevent Step-4 deterministic drift where identical Step-2/Step-3 evidence is rescored with a recomputed taskEcho profile (e.g., none -> moderate), especially on LR6 boundary rows.
hypothesis.guardExpectation: No scoring behavior change when Step-2 taskEcho is missing; recomputation path remains active for incomplete payloads.
hypothesis.lowBandExpectation: No regression in low-band activation/followup/polarity suites.

entries.notes: Patch remains narrow and non-architectural: only taskEcho signal source priority changed (preserve complete incoming Step-2 payload first, recompute only when missing).

entries.criterionFocus: LR6-1 one-row single-part coverage-signal boundary recovery for high-content anchored responses

entries.notes: Path remains narrow: requires single-part coverage-signal-thin shape, anchored non-contradictory stance, strong body/topic support, long effective content, clean prompt echo, and controlled repetition.
